body.title: export_chat.py: route each message to its own day-file (fix cross-day session bloat)
body.result: Per-message date routing + union-of-all-sessions rewrites. Fixed first-line-no-timestamp fallback-to-mtime bug. 16 unit tests. Idempotent by construction. Commit 571c91f6.
body.description:
lab/claudecode/export_chat.py currently writes one session to one day-file via _date_for_transcript (first-message timestamp, else mtime fallback). Long-lived sessions spanning days dump ALL content into ONE day, and sessions whose first line lacks a timestamp (permission-mode / file-history-snapshot records) fall back to mtime → always route to 'today.' Evidence: session 86b1b97d started 2026-04-18, still active 2026-04-22, entire 4-day content in 2026-04-22.md; 2026-04-18.md is 297MB of likely-duplicated same content from prior --all runs.

Affected files:
- lab/claudecode/export_chat.py:90 _date_for_transcript (first-line-only logic)
- lab/claudecode/export_chat.py:107 render (session-level rendering)
- lab/claudecode/export_chat.py:130 write_out (single-file output)

Design rules:
- Chat log files reflect the DAY, not the session lifecycle
- Idempotent under --all re-runs (running twice produces same content, no duplication)
- A session can write to N day-files

Scope:
- Partition messages by their OWN timestamp → render per-day blocks
- For message without timestamp, attach to the previous-timestamped message's day
- Output path per partition: claude_chat_logs/YYYY-MM-DD.md
- Idempotency under --all: rebuild each day-file as union of all messages dated that day across all sessions (rewrite, not append-with-separator)

Out of scope (separate tickets if wanted):
- Historical cleanup of duplicated content in existing day-files (note: 2026-04-18.md at 297MB is likely bloated)
- The first-msg-of-day prompt (T-first-msg-of-day-close-prompt)

Test plan:
- Unit: cross-day jsonl fixture (messages spanning 3 days) → verify split across 3 day-files
- Unit: first-line-without-timestamp fixture → verify fallback scans to next-timestamped line
- Unit: run render twice on same fixture, verify idempotent output
- Integration: run --all on real transcript dir, spot-check a long-lived session lands its content in correct days
